'''Blake Taaffe''' (born 21 April 1999) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Early life==
Taaffe was educated at Tuggerah Lakes Secondary College.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Playing career==

===2021===
In round 15 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Taaffe made his first grade debut for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in a 46–0 victory.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-06-17/nrl-south-sydney-rabbitohs-thrash-brisbane-broncos/100215554/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs thrash Brisbane Broncos 46-0, Payne Haas on report|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 June 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-blake-taaffe-debut-south-sydney-rabbitohs-vs-brisbane-broncos-round-15/news-story/c466d6d43927648562d712799c04bc2c/|title=Souths set to unleash latest rookie sensation as cover for Latrell|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=16 June 2021}}</ref>

Taaffe played a total of eight games for South Sydney in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's [[2021 NRL Grand Final]] defeat against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/oct/04/one-of-finest-nrl-grand-finals-of-all-time-provides-cure-to-difficult-season/|title=One of finest NRL grand finals of all time provides cure to difficult season|website=www.theguardian.com|date=3 October 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Taaffe played a total of ten games for South Sydney in the [[2022 NRL season]]. Taaffe did not feature in any of South Sydney's finals matches as the club reached the preliminary final before losing to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-09-24/nrl-penrith-panthers-defeats-south-sydney-rabbitohs/101470088/|title=Penrith Panthers defeat South Sydney Rabbitohs 32-12 to qualify for third straight NRL grand final|newspaper=ABC News|date=24 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
In round 19 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Taaffe scored two tries for South Sydney in their 36-32 loss against Canterbury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/live-nrl-bulldogs-unveil-sixth-starting-halves-combo-of-season-against-undermanned-rabbitohs/news-story/9200c30ee3f9d86aca17687d198801d0/|title=Youngsters shining at Accor as Dogs rookie winger's third try puts game to bed - LIVE|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 July 2023 }}</ref>

On 31 July, Taaffe signed a two-year deal to join Canterbury starting in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-contract-news-blake-taaffe-joins-canterbury-bulldogs/xd2lazd9it8ljfeaotjouqdn/|title=Blake Taaffe joins Canterbury on a two year deal|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

On 24 September, Taaffe played for South Sydney in their 2023 NSW Cup grand final victory over [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]]. Taaffe was named as man of the match for his performance in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-blake-taaffe-zach-dockarclay-dean-hawkins-south-sydney-rabbitohs-north-sydney-bears-nsw-cup-grand-final-stats-result/news-story/befffd38099aec69314f2ff2171b9376/|title=Taaffe's statement as Souths end 40-year drought; veteran's captain's knock: Reserves Wrap|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
In round 1 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Taaffe made his club debut for Canterbury in their 28-6 loss against arch-rivals [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-parramatta-eels-vs-canterbury-bulldogs-scores-updates-live-blog-replays-stephen-crichton-mitch-moses-cameron-ciraldo/news-story/0aefa4d6f143032275f8a1186326e209/|title=Dogs' Foxx fear compounds rough night; Eels may have finally solved big issue: What we learned|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

Taaffe played a total of nine games for Canterbury in the 2024 NRL season as they qualified for the finals finishing 6th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review-phil-gould-cameron-ciraldo-20240916-p5kb0n.html/|title=Bulldogs left 'frustrated' by semi final defeat as new standards laid bare|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 25 August, Taaffe signed a three-year deal to join the [[Castleford Tigers]] from 2026.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=13 August 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers launch transfer move for Canterbury Bulldogs fullback |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-transfer-nrl-canterbury-bulldogs-blake-taaffe |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=25 August 2025 |title=Tigers sign Blake Taaffe on three-year deal |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article/47/tigers-sign-blake-taaffe-on-three-year-deal |access-date=26 August 2025 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Taaffe was limited to only four games with Canterbury in the [[2025 NRL season]] and did not feature in their finals campaign.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-exclusive-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review-20250922-p5mx5l.html/|title=Clue to what went horribly wrong for Bulldogs emerges in 'unhappy ending'|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
Taaffe was assigned Castleford's number 1 shirt for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=6 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers' Blake Taaffe talks passionate fans, pride in the shirt & hitting ground running v Doncaster |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-blake-taaffe-talks-passionate-fans-pride-in-the-shirt-hitting-ground-running-v-doncaster-5506991 |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> He scored a try on debut on 8 February against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> In the opening round of [[2026 Super League season|Super League]] against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], Taaffe suffered a knee injury in the second half;<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brocksom |first=Sam |date=15 February 2026 |title=Keighran sees yellow, Eckersley try double, Blake Taaffe concern and more Castleford Tigers v Wigan Warriors talking points |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-keighran-sees-yellow-eckersley-try-double-blake-taaffe-concern-and-more-castleford-tigers-v-wigan-warriors-talking-points-112704/ |access-date=19 February 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> he was later confirmed to have suffered a [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|ruptured ACL]], thus ruling him out for the entire remainder of the year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 February 2026 |title=Castleford's Taaffe to miss rest of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c9v0yxl2mk8o |access-date=19 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=17 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers rocked by huge injury blow for star signing Blake Taaffe |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-rocked-by-huge-injury-blow-for-star-signing-blake-taaffe-5602011 |access-date=19 February 2026 |website=Wakefield
